title: ranunculus aquatilis var. caespitosus
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis var. caespitosus, also known as the tufted water buttercup, is a variety of flowering plant that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its alkaloids and glycosides. Consuming the plant may provide health benefits, including reduced inflammation and improved wound healing, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. In herbal preparations, Ranunculus aquatilis var. caespitosus is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves, although caution should be exercised due to its potential toxicity and side effects.
